install.WARNING: The npm package name is EXACTLY '@qwen-code/qwen-code'. Do NOT install 'qwen-code' or 'qwen' alone (wrong packages). The CLI binary that lands on PATH is `qwen` (not `qwen-code`).
install.note: Qwen Code is Alibaba's open-source coding agent CLI (a fork of Gemini CLI tuned for Qwen models). Requires Node.js ≥ 20. After install, verify with `qwen --version`. Config lives at ~/.qwen/settings.json — Echobird's 'Update model config' flow writes it for you.

agent_steps: Detect OS and verify Node.js ≥ 20 with `node --version`. If missing or older, install Node via nvm / winget OpenJS.NodeJS / brew install node first.
Run `npm install -g @qwen-code/qwen-code` (or `bun add -g @qwen-code/qwen-code` if the user prefers bun).
After install, run `qwen --version` to confirm. The CLI binary is `qwen`, not `qwen-code` — point this out to avoid confusion.
Tell the user that model selection happens via Echobird's App Manager — clicking 'Update model config' writes ~/.qwen/settings.json with the chosen base URL / API key / model.
tell_user: Node.js prerequisite (and how to install if missing)
That the CLI binary is `qwen`, not `qwen-code`
That ~/.qwen/settings.json is the config file Echobird writes when applying a model

identity_guard.description: Qwen Code is Alibaba/QwenLM's open-source coding agent CLI (`@qwen-code/qwen-code` on npm, QwenLM/qwen-code on GitHub). It is a Qwen-optimized fork of Google's Gemini CLI. It is NOT 'Qwen Coder' (the model itself) and NOT 'Qwen-Agent' (a different agent framework) — they are separate products that happen to share the Qwen brand.
